question: Is the model in the black blazer noticeably smaller (approximately 20% reduced in scale) compared to the original image?
Has the model in the black blazer been shifted to the right, creating a visible separation from the other figure?
Is there distinct 'breathing room' or a visible gap between the two figures, ensuring they are no longer crowded or overlapping?
Does the resized model in the black blazer maintain correct anatomical proportions (head-to-body ratio, limb lengths) without distortion?
Is the original pose (including hand-in-pocket) and facial expression of the model in the black blazer preserved after resizing?
Does the model in the red dress remain at her original size, position, and clarity without distortion?
Is the newly exposed background area between the figures filled with floral textures (roses, hydrangeas, foliage) that are consistent with the surrounding arch?
Is the side of the red dress, which was previously occluded by the black blazer, reconstructed naturally without artifacts?
Are the 'MODERN MUSE' masthead and 'THE SYMMETRY ISSUE' subtitle text preserved without distortion or displacement?
Are the background architectural elements (arched windows) and unedited parts of the floral arch preserved unchanged?
Are the facial features, hair, and clothing texture of the resized model sharp and distinct, avoiding blurriness or pixelation?
Does the repositioned model appear grounded with consistent lighting and shadows, avoiding a 'floating' appearance?
Is the image free of editing artifacts such as visible seams, halos around the subject, or extra/floating limbs?
